description:
A small data-engineering shop just adopted Git-ops for managing their
    ETL configuration files.  
    You are given a working copy that already contains the directory
    /home/user/etl with two sub-directories:

      • /home/user/etl/config  
      • /home/user/etl/logs  

    Right now there is **one** configuration file that still has the
    old settings:

    └─ /home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml  

    Its current contents are

        version: 1
        steps:
          extract:
            source: "s3://raw-data"
          transform:
            script: "transform.py"
          load:
            destination: "warehouse"

    The lead engineer has provided the *desired* version of the same
    file (shown below).  
    Your task is to:

    1. Create a **unified diff** (a “patch” file) that transforms the
       existing pipeline.yml into the desired version.  
       • Save the diff as
         /home/user/etl/logs/pipeline_update.diff  
       • The diff **must** start with the exact two header
         lines shown here (no spaces before them):

           --- a/pipeline.yml
           +++ b/pipeline.yml

       • The diff must use standard “@@ … @@” hunk markers and
         contain **only** the minimal line changes needed.

    2. Apply the patch you just produced so that
       /home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml becomes identical to the
       desired version.

    3. Append the single line

           [OK] pipeline.yml patched 2024-01-01 00:00:00

       to the file /home/user/etl/logs/update.log  
       (create the log file if it does not yet exist).

    Desired final contents of pipeline.yml:

        version: 2
        steps:
          extract:
            source: "s3://processed-data"
          validate:
            script: "validate.py"
          transform:
            script: "transform.py"
          load:
            destination: "warehouse"

    Notes & constraints
    -------------------
    • Relative vs absolute paths: the patch headers should
      reference just “a/pipeline.yml” and “b/pipeline.yml”, but the
      patch must be **applied** to the real file located at
      /home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml.  
    • Do **not** hard-code any temporary directory paths inside the
      diff.  
    • The date–time string in update.log **must appear exactly** as
      shown above so the automated grader can match it literally.  
    • Only standard command-line tools available in every basic Linux
      distribution may be used (e.g., diff, patch, echo, cat, etc.).

    Success criteria checked by the grader
    --------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/etl/logs/pipeline_update.diff exists and starts
       with the required two header lines.  
    2. The diff, when re-applied in an empty copy of the starting
       tree, recreates the desired file without error.  
    3. /home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml is byte-for-byte identical
       to the desired version shown above.  
    4. /home/user/etl/logs/update.log ends with the exact line

           [OK] pipeline.yml patched 2024-01-01 00:00:00

       (including brackets and spacing, ending with a newline).
truth:
<!-- Initial file present before the agent starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml"><![CDATA[
version: 1
steps:
  extract:
    source: "s3://raw-data"
  transform:
    script: "transform.py"
  load:
    destination: "warehouse"
]]></initial_file>

    <!-- Expected final state of the same file -->
    <final_file path="/home/user/etl/config/pipeline.yml"><![CDATA[
version: 2
steps:
  extract:
    source: "s3://processed-data"
  validate:
    script: "validate.py"
  transform:
    script: "transform.py"
  load:
    destination: "warehouse"
]]></final_file>

    <!-- Expected exact contents of the diff file the agent must create -->
    <final_file path="/home/user/etl/logs/pipeline_update.diff"><![CDATA[
--- a/pipeline.yml
+++ b/pipeline.yml
@@ -1,8 +1,10 @@
-version: 1
+version: 2
 steps:
   extract:
-    source: "s3://raw-data"
+    source: "s3://processed-data"
+  validate:
+    script: "validate.py"
   transform:
     script: "transform.py"
   load:
     destination: "warehouse"
]]></final_file>

    <!-- Expected final contents of the update log -->
    <final_file path="/home/user/etl/logs/update.log"><![CDATA[
[OK] pipeline.yml patched 2024-01-01 00:00:00
]]></final_file>
name: task_000000_193ec7f9
